title: Not Easy To Find, But Well Worth The Effort.
text:
If you're interested in the history of Botswana & Bechuanaland, then this quaint little Museum is well worth a visit. We visited while passing through Serowe on our way to the Khama Rhino Sanctuary. It's not very well signposted, but if you take the time to find it, you're in for a fascinating hour or so with a local Guide showing you around, explaining the fascinating history, and, if you're lucky, maybe getting a live rendition of a traditional song thrown in for good measure.
We absolutely loved our time here, and would recommend it to anyone with an interest in the Country's history, or the story of Sir Seretse Khama & Ruth (as depicted in the film "A United Kingdom").
And, just like over the rest of Botswana, the People were wonderful and so welcoming.
